2026-03-14.title: Sleep Science This Week: March 8-14, 2026
changes: Updated 'Protect your memory and cognitive function' in sleep_apnea section - Sleep (Oxford Academic, March 2026) perspective on CPAP potentially enhancing glymphatic/CSF waste clearance and modifying neurodegenerative risk

Updated 'Understand the double impact on your heart' in sleep_apnea section - Sleep (Oxford Academic, March 2026) found hypoxemia and arousals create distinct cardiac response signatures; phenotyping as hypoxemic vs arousal dominant may guide personalized treatment

Added 'Request lipoprotein(a) testing if you have OSA and heart disease' tip in sleep_apnea section - Sleep (Oxford Academic, March 2026) found OSA plus elevated Lp(a) in heart attack survivors creates high-risk combination with worse cardiovascular outcomes

Added 'Ask about cardiac phenotyping during sleep studies' tip in sleep_apnea section - Sleep (Oxford Academic, March 2026) showed oxygen desaturation vs arousals produce distinct RR interval patterns; detailed cardiac monitoring could guide risk stratification

2026-03-07.title: Sleep Science This Week: March 1-7, 2026
changes: Added 'Request gradual CPAP pressure titration if you're 65 or older' tip in sleep_apnea section - Liu et al. (Nature and Science of Sleep, March 2026) found older adults show higher arousal, HRV changes, and reduced slow-wave sleep at higher pressures during titration

Added 'Discuss metabolic screening for heart disease and sleep apnea' tip in sleep_apnea section - Chen et al. identified blood biomarkers (D-glucuronolactone, tryptophan) for CADOSA non-invasive detection

Added 'Ask about airway remodeling markers if CPAP isn't working' tip in sleep_apnea section - Yen-Chun Chen et al. (Journal of Sleep Research, March 2026) identified epithelial Na+/K+ imbalance as surface markers of airway remodeling

Added 'Seek multidisciplinary PAP programs for pediatric OSA' tip in children section - Linares et al. (Frontiers in Sleep, March 2026) Brazilian interdisciplinary program improved pediatric PAP adherence

2026-02-28.title: Sleep Science This Week: February 22-28, 2026
changes: Added 'Help children overcome nightmares using cognitive-behavioral tools' tip in children section - DARC-NESS mastery-based CBT framework (Cromer et al., Frontiers in Sleep, Feb 27 2026) showed nightmare frequency reduction from 4/week to zero; introduces nightmare efficacy as central mechanism, with worry boxes, belly breathing, positive sleep cues, and nightmare rescripting as key techniques

2026-02-21.title: Sleep Science This Week: February 15-21, 2026
changes: Added 'Get screened for sleep apnea after COVID-19 recovery' tip in sleep_apnea section - cohort of 910,393 patients showed elevated new-onset OSA risk up to 4.5 years post-infection (HR 1.33 non-hospitalized, HR 1.41 hospitalized)

Added 'Check vitamin D status if you have sleep apnea' tip in sleep_apnea section - matched cohort (n=36,497) linked vitamin D deficiency with higher heart failure risk (HR 1.45), all-cause mortality (HR 1.76), and pulmonary embolism risk

Added 'Stay current on respiratory vaccines if you have OSA' tip in sleep_apnea section - mortality trend data showed substantial long-term increase in pneumonia-related deaths among patients with OSA
Added 'Screen for sleep apnea in chronic low back pain' tip in sleep_apnea section - 2026 study linked OSA risk with accelerated aging biomarkers (PhenoAge, DunedinPACE) and worse pain outcomes
Updated 'Ask about patch-type or wearable OSA screening' in sleep_apnea section with Feb 2026 transformer-based single-lead ECG evidence (F1 0.863, AUC 0.852) for second-by-second apnea detection

2026-02-14.title: Sleep Science This Week: February 8-14, 2026
changes: Updated 'Advocate for faster, coordinated care' in sleep_apnea section with long-term pathway outcomes (84.3% PAP adoption, 80.6% 90-day adherence, 74.2% adherence at 2 years)

Added new sleep_apnea tip: 'Get sleep and swallowing evaluation after head/neck cancer treatment' to reflect new evidence linking nocturnal swallowing dysfunction, aspiration risk, and OSA in treated HNC patients

2026-02-07.title: Sleep Protocol Tips Update: February 7, 2026
changes: Added 'Ask about patch-type or wearable OSA screening' tip in sleep_apnea section - AI-powered chest patch achieved 81.4% accuracy for moderate-to-severe OSA, can monitor up to one week

Added 'Try therapist-guided online mindfulness therapy for chronic insomnia' tip in schedule section - 8-week iMBCT-I showed 56.10% remission vs 17.07% for sleep hygiene, benefits at 20-week follow-up

Added 'Seek early sleep-behavior intervention for preschoolers with ADHD signs' tip in children section - PASS protocol combines parent training with sleep medicine; sleep problems affect 2-in-3 young children with ADHD symptoms

2026-01-31.title: Sleep Science This Week: January 24-30, 2026
changes: Updated 'Watch for blood clot warning signs' in sleep_apnea section - Added Mendelian randomization evidence that obesity, not OSA itself, may be the primary driver of blood clotting problems (study of 790 OSA patients)

Added 'Request formal sleep testing after stroke or TIA' tip in sleep_apnea section - Up to 70% of stroke patients have undiagnosed OSA, and standard questionnaires (STOP-BANG, Berlin, etc.) don't perform well enough in this population

Added 'Request earplugs during surgery for better recovery sleep' tip in sleep_apnea section - RCT of 90 patients showing earplugs improved post-op sleep quality, reduced propofol use by 20%, and shortened hospital stays
Updated 'Lose weight to reduce sleep apnea severity' in weight_management section - Added evidence that weight loss may reduce thrombotic risk more effectively than CPAP alone

2026-01-23.title: Sleep Protocol Tips Update: January 23, 2026
changes: Added 'Ask about newer PAP technologies if CPAP is uncomfortable' tip in sleep_apnea section (KPAP showing 50% less mask leak and 69-84% patient preference over traditional CPAP)
Added 'Get screened for sleep apnea if you have COPD' tip in sleep_apnea section (overlap syndrome affects 28-30% of COPD patients with worse outcomes requiring BiPAP)
Added 'Consider GLP-1 medications for obesity-related sleep apnea' tip in sleep_apnea section (emerging evidence for semaglutide/tirzepatide in managing metabolic component of OSA)

Updated 'Consider histamine-targeted treatment for resistant insomnia' with additional case evidence (74-year-old with 10 months treatment-resistant insomnia recovered in 24 hours with antihistamine therapy)

2026-01-16.title: Sleep Protocol Tips Update: January 16, 2026
changes: Added 'Prioritize sleep efficiency over total time in bed' tip in schedule section (study of 173 adults showing sleep efficiency <85% linked to worse memory, especially in older adults)
Added 'Try video-based sleep education before medication' tip in schedule section (RCT of 119 adults aged 50-80 showing 8-minute video improved subjective sleep quality without medication)
Added 'Put away your phone during your child's bedtime routine' tip in children section (study of 1,295 preschoolers showing parental smartphone use during bedtime linked to ~19 min shorter sleep)
Added 'Consider separate sleep spaces for better child sleep quality' tip in children section (co-sleeping associated with more night wakings and daytime sleepiness)

Updated 'Understand long-term risk tradeoffs of sleep medications' in pharmacology section (added nuance that non-GABAergic sleep medications may actually support memory, unlike benzodiazepines/Z-drugs)

2026-01-09.title: Sleep Science This Week: January 3-9, 2026
changes: Added telemedicine and AI-enabled OSA care tip (home sleep testing with automated scoring, virtual PAP setup, adherence prediction, and LLM-supported patient education)

Added pediatric clinical-cephalometric screening tip for resource-limited settings (clinical signs plus ANB angle and nasopharyngeal soft tissue ratio ≥0.68 capturing ~90% of moderate-severe cases with 86% accuracy)
Expanded evening blue-light guidance with mechanistic evidence (450–465 nm exposure damaging retinal structure and disrupting melatonin/circadian signaling via TGF-beta, JAK-STAT, MAPK pathways)

2026-01-03.title: Additional categories added to the Sleep Protocol
changes: Added new 'circadian' category with 4 tips: Morning light exposure, evening blue light suppression, seasonal photoperiod alignment, and geographic latitude adjustments - Based on research showing light timing is critical for circadian rhythm regulation

Added new 'temperature' category with 3 tips: Core body temperature timing, bedroom temperature optimization, and pre-sleep cooling/post-wake warming strategies - Research shows temperature regulation is essential for sleep onset and quality

Added new 'neurochemical' category with 4 tips: Caffeine cutoff logic, alcohol timing/exclusion, nicotine impact, and supplement timing (magnesium, glycine) - Covers neurochemical factors affecting sleep

Added new 'chronotype' category with 4 tips: Chronotype identification, shift work adaptation, travel/jet lag protocols, and social jet lag mitigation - Evidence-based personalization for individual circadian rhythms
Added new 'cognitive' category with 3 tips: Stress downregulation, rumination control, and anxiety/hyperarousal management - Addresses cognitive and emotional factors affecting sleep
Added new 'digital' category with 4 tips: Screen timing rules, content intensity control, dopamine load management, and notification suppression windows - Digital hygiene for better sleep

Added new 'measurement' category with 4 tips: Sleep stages optimization, HRV/RHR/respiratory rate tracking, longitudinal trend analysis, and intervention-to-outcome feedback loops - Data-driven sleep optimization
Added new 'environment' category with 4 tips: Noise profiles, air quality (CO₂/VOCs), humidity control, and mattress/pillow biomechanical fit - Comprehensive environmental optimization
Added new 'pharmacology' category with 3 tips: Short-term sleep aids, long-term risk tradeoffs, and dependency avoidance frameworks - Safe use of sleep medications
Added new 'recovery' category with 3 tips: Training-sleep coupling, illness/overtraining adjustments, and cognitive performance carryover - Integration of sleep with performance and recovery
Added new 'aging' category with 3 tips: Sleep architecture changes with age, REM/deep sleep preservation, and neurodegeneration risk mitigation - Age-specific sleep considerations
Added new 'governance' category with 4 tips: Non-negotiables vs optional layers, failure modes, compliance scoring, and iteration cadence - Protocol management and optimization

2026-01-02.title: Sleep Science This Week: Dec 27, 2025 - Jan 2, 2026
changes: Updated 'Screen overweight children for sleep apnea early' - Added specific statistics: 22.7% of children have undiagnosed OSA, overweight = 2.71x risk, obesity = 2.80x risk, weight gain since 18 months linked to higher risk

Updated 'Understand adenoid issues in children with OSA' - Replaced with ferroptosis mechanism research showing reduced cell death allows tissue overgrowth, identified 7 hub genes as potential therapeutic targets

Added new tip: 'Consider comprehensive, team-based care for children' - Emphasizes multidisciplinary programs combining nutrition, exercise, psychology, and sleep medicine for children with obesity and sleep problems
Added new tip: 'Track your child's weight trajectory from early age' - Highlights that weight gain since 18 months increases OSA risk, emphasizing early weight management
Added new tip: 'Explore digital sleep interventions for children' - Discusses mobile health apps showing promise for pediatric insomnia treatment
Added new tip: 'Monitor daytime functioning for sleep-disordered breathing signs' - Helps parents recognize behavioral and academic warning signs of undiagnosed OSA in children

2025-12-26.title: Sleep Science This Week: December 20-26, 2025
changes: Added new tip: 'Advocate for faster, coordinated care' - Based on research showing streamlined care improves CPAP adherence from 11% to 74%
Added new tip: 'Watch for blood clot warning signs' - Highlights the overlooked VTE risk in sleep apnea patients
Added new tip: 'Understand the double impact on your heart' - Explains how low oxygen AND sleep disruptions compound cardiovascular stress
Added new tip: 'Protect your memory and cognitive function' - Links OSA treatment to dementia prevention through sleep spindle protection
Added new tip: 'Understand night-to-night OSA variability' - Research shows OSA severity can vary dramatically, single-night studies may be unreliable
Updated 'Use your CPAP machine every night' - Added information about comprehensive care programs improving adherence
Updated 'Maintain a consistent sleep schedule' - Added research suggesting timing shifts may be less harmful than total sleep loss
Updated 'Lose weight to reduce sleep apnea severity' - Added research on childhood obesity-OSA connection starting as early as 18 months
Updated 'Exercise regularly to improve sleep quality' - Added details about exercise providing short-term protection but not preventing long-term heart damage from sleep deprivation
Added new 'children' category with two tips: 'Screen overweight children for sleep apnea early' and 'Understand adenoid issues in children with OSA'

2025-12-19.title: Sleep Science This Week: December 13-19, 2025
changes: Added new tip: 'Consider histamine-targeted treatment for resistant insomnia' - Based on research showing mast cell-driven inflammation may cause treatment-resistant insomnia
Added new tip: 'Establish age-appropriate bedtimes for teenagers' - Research shows sleep timing (not just duration) affects teen mental health and suicide risk
Added new tip: 'Avoid quick returns in shift work schedules' - Highlights the dangers of less than 11 hours between shifts
Added new tip: 'Consider your chronotype for important activities' - Evening types genuinely perform worse in morning, not just psychological preference
